result:
Done — all three deliverables are in place and verified.

**What was produced** (in `runs/fable/t1/`):
- `script.R` — self-contained: Okabe-Ito palette and `theme_minimal`-based theme declared at the top, `set.seed(42)` first, loads `exampleData1`, reshapes with `reshape_projoint`, prints the design summary and balance tables, and saves the figure at 300 dpi (3000×2700 px).
- `summary.md` — 400 respondents, 8 tasks each plus 1 repeated (flipped) task-1 for reliability, 2 profiles per task; all 7 attributes with human-readable names and levels (Housing Cost 3, Presidential Vote 3, Racial Composition 4, School Quality 2, Driving Time 4, Type of Place 6, Violent Crime Rate 2); balance check over the 6,400 profile-attribute assignments shows every attribute within ~3.1 pp of uniform (most within 1 pp), so randomization looks sound. Figure caption sits under the image reference.
- `figures/level-frequencies.png` — one faceted horizontal bar chart of level shares, Okabe-Ito colored, no in-plot title.

**Orchestration record:** as planned, the whole task routed to the fast-worker (Sonnet) as one mechanical unit — 1 of 3 delegations used. My integration check found one defect: two facet strip titles were clipped in the figure. I spent the single revision cycle fixing it myself (added `label_wrap_gen(width = 32)` to `facet_wrap`) rather than re-delegating, re-ran `Rscript script.R` (exit 0), and confirmed visually that all facet titles now render in full. The native advisor tool was unavailable this session, so the verification relied on my direct re-run and inspection.
